title: Model–Task Capability Map
one_liner: Route tasks by verified suitability, not by what's cheapest or fastest.
problem: Multi-model setups make routing accidental: tasks land on whichever model is available or familiar. A model good at writing may be weak at code, unfit for high-impact reasoning.
solution: Maintain an explicit map of which models are approved for which task categories — backed by benchmarks, internal evals, regression tests, incident history. Describes what the system is willing to let a model do, not what it could theoretically do.
